category.name: Comments
category.preferred_workflow: Use native Aspose.Words APIs directly. Prefer simple, verifiable operations over ambitious markup-preservation tricks. Create realistic local sample inputs inside the example whenever external files, folders, streams, or templates are required.

strict_api_bans: Do not use Document.Comments. Enumerate comments with doc.GetChildNodes(NodeType.Comment, true).OfType<Comment>().
Do not invent an Aspose.Words.Comments namespace or any invented comment collection namespace.
Do not invent DocumentBuilder.StartComment, DocumentBuilder.EndComment, AddComment, InsertComment, or similar builder shortcut APIs unless the exact Aspose.Words API is known and supported.
Do not use CommentReference in this category.
canonical_patterns: Append at least one Paragraph and Run inside a Comment when creating comment content.
Use safe ToList copies before deleting or bulk-updating comments.
For report/export scenarios, extract plain data first and recreate destination content from that data.
For stream workflows, reset MemoryStream.Position before reloading or resaving.
nullable_warning_prevention: Avoid CS8600, CS8602, and CS8604 by checking for null before assignment, dereference, indexing, or method calls.
Do not assign maybe-null values directly to non-nullable variables.
Declare nullable locals such as Paragraph? when a value may legitimately be null.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: error CS1061: 'Node' does not contain a definition for 'Text'
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use Comment.GetText() for whole-comment content, or cast to a concrete node type such as Run before reading .Text.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Compile failures caused by Document.Comments or Aspose.Words.Comments usage.
Compile failures caused by CommentReference or undocumented save-option flags.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Prefer simpler comment workflows and documented Aspose.Words save options only.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Unstable behavior while deleting comments during forward iteration over a live collection.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create a ToList copy first, then update or remove matching comments.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: CS8600, CS8602, or CS8604 around maybe-null values such as CurrentParagraph or FirstOrDefault results.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use nullable locals where needed and guard maybe-null values before dereference or assignment.

examples.task: Add a comment containing a hyperlink to an external resource and verify the link functions in rendered output.
Add a new comment to a specific paragraph in a word document and save as docx.
Apply a custom style to all comment text blocks within a document to match corporate branding.
Compare two versions of a document and list comments that were added modified or deleted.
Convert a doc file to pdf while retaining all comment annotations visible in the output.
Convert a document with comments to XPS format, ensuring comments appear as markup annotations.
Create a batch process that adds a standardized disclaimer comment to every document in a.
Create a reply to an existing comment and ensure the reply appears nested under the original comment.
Create a utility that reads comment data from a database and inserts corresponding comments into a document.
Delete all comments authored by a particular user from the loaded document before exporting.
Export all comments from a docx file to a csv file with author date and text columns.
Extract comment metadata author date and text and write it to a json file.
Extract comment text and embed it as footnotes within the same document for alternative presentation.
Filter comments by author and export only those comments to a separate Word document for review.
Generate a printable report listing all comments with page numbers and associated paragraphs.
Implement a feature that hides all comments in the document view without removing them from the document.
Import comments from an exported XML file and attach them to appropriate locations in a new document.
Iterate through the comment collection and remove comments older than a specified date threshold.
Load a document change comment author names to uppercase and save the updated file.
Load a document from a stream, add comments, and save the modified document back to a memory stream.
Load a docx file enumerate all comments and print each author and text to console.
Load multiple Word documents from a folder, aggregate their comments, and generate a summary report.
Preserve comment formatting such as bold and italic text when converting a document to HTML.
Programmatically accept or reject comments based on author name and generate a revised document version.
Search comments containing a specific keyword and highlight the corresponding text range in the document.
Set custom author name and initials for programmatically added comments in a document.
Synchronize comment positions after document sections are reordered to maintain accurate comment placement.
Update the text of an existing comment identified by its index while preserving original formatting.
Use comment collection events or equivalent wrapper-based logging to capture add and remove operations.
Validate that comment reference IDs update correctly after inserting new paragraphs into the document.
